programs.name: Mississippi Medicaid Elderly and Disabled Waiver
programs.what_it_does: The Elderly and Disabled (E&D) Waiver is a statewide Medicaid program providing home and community-based services to prevent nursing home institutionalization. Services include case management, adult day care, home delivered meals, personal care, respite care, and transition assistance tailored to individual needs.[1][3]
programs.who_its_for: Mississippi residents aged 65+ or 21-64 who are physically disabled, require nursing home level of care, and are Medicaid eligible.[1][3]
programs.benefit_value: Limited enrollment slots with waitlist when full; services vary by need but promote independent living as alternative to institutional care.[1]

programs.name: Mississippi State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: SHIP provides free counseling on Medicare, Medicaid, supplemental insurance, long-term care options, and help with claims, appeals, and policy comparisons. Volunteers assist older adults and caregivers in understanding benefits and organizing paperwork.[4][6]
programs.who_its_for: Mississippi Medicare beneficiaries, older adults, caregivers, and those with questions on health insurance including Medicare Savings Programs.[4][6]
programs.benefit_value: Free personalized assistance statewide through Area Agencies on Aging; no financial benefit but saves on premiums and out-of-pocket costs via guidance.[4]

programs.name: State Long Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: The program resolves problems for long-term care facility residents and advocates for their rights to improve quality of life. Services are provided through contracts with Area Agencies on Aging using staff and volunteers.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Residents of long-term care facilities in Mississippi.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Advocacy and problem resolution at no cost; enhances resident rights and care quality.[3]

programs.name: Mississippi Access to Care (MAC) Network
programs.what_it_does: MAC Centers provide free, unbiased, and confidential assistance to locate long-term care services and supports, helping individuals remain independent in the community. They offer information on a broad range of programs, help evaluate options, and assist with applications for benefits.[4][8]
programs.who_its_for: Older Mississippians, people with disabilities, their families or representatives, and anyone seeking long-term care assistance regardless of income.[4][8]
programs.whats_unique: State-specific network of centers dedicated to connecting residents to Mississippi's long-term services and supports, serving as a central, accessible hub not universally replicated in other states.[6][8]
programs.benefit_value: Free information, assistance, and navigation services; no direct financial value but enables access to other benefits.[8]

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: This program offers subsidized, part-time community service work experience to help seniors gain or enhance skills for reentering the workforce. It is administered across all 82 Mississippi counties through area agencies on aging and non-profits.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed Mississippians aged 55+ seeking to reenter the workforce.[3]
programs.whats_unique: Mississippi's state-administered implementation under Title V of the Older Americans Act, providing localized training in every county tailored to the state's needs.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Subsidized part-time work experience for a limited time; wage details not specified but aimed at skill-building for permanent employment.[3]
